Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org|publisher=rugbyleagueproject.org|date=31 December 2017|access-date=1 January 2018}} |updated = 16 June 2018 }} '''James Lowes''' is a [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] [[Coach (sport)|coach]], and a former professional rugby league footballer. He has been senior performance coach with [[Halifax Panthers]] since 2025. He played rugby league for [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]], and found most success with [[Bradford Bulls]], from 1996 to 2003. He won the [[Man of Steel Award]] in 1997. He won one international cap for [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]], and five for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]]. In rugby league, he has had coaching roles with Salford, [[Warrington Wolves]], Bradford Bulls and Leeds Rhinos, England and Great Britain. In rugby union, he has coached Leeds/Yorkshire Carnegie, Ealing Trailfinders and West Park Leeds. ==Club career== Lowes began his career at [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet]], joining from junior club [[Hunslet Club Parkside|Hunslet Parkside]] in October 1986.{{cite book |editor-last1=Fletcher |editor-first1=Raymond |editor-last2=Howes |editor-first2=David |title=Rothmans Rugby League Yearbook 1987-88 |date=1987 |publisher=Queen Anne Press |location=London |isbn=978-0-356-14620-1 |page=368}} He was signed by [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in 1992 for an initial transfer fee of £30,000.{{cite web|last1=Hadfield|first1=Dave|title=Rugby League: Lydon to step in for Steadman|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-league-lydon-to-step-in-for-steadman-1558973.html|website=The Independent|access-date=1 March 2016|date=22 October 1992}} Lowes had usually played as a [[stand-off (rugby league)|stand-off]] or [[scrum-half (rugby league)|scrum-half]] during his early career, but was converted into a [[hooker (rugby league)|hooker]] when he joined Leeds. Lowes moved to [[Bradford Bulls]] for the inaugural [[Super League]] season in 1996, following the expiry of his contract at Leeds. Lowes was the [[1997 Bradford Bulls season]]'s top [[Try (rugby)|try]] scorer, and won the [[Man of Steel Award]] as the Bradford Bulls claimed their first Super League championship. In the 1997 post-season Lowes was selected to play for [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] at hooker in all three matches against [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]] in the [[Super League Test series]], scoring a try in the opening game. Lowes played for [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|hk}} in the [[1999 Super League Grand Final]] which was lost to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. Lowes played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|hk}}, and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their [[2001 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]]. As [[Super League VI]] champions, the Bradford Bulls played against [[2001 NRL season|2001 NRL]] Premiers, the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[2002 World Club Challenge]]. Lowes played at hooker in Bradford's victory. Lowes played for [[Bradford Bulls]] at {{rlp|hk}} in their [[2002 Super League Grand Final]] loss against [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]]. Lowes played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] at hooker and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in their [[2003 Super League Grand Final]] victory against the [[Wigan Warriors]], which was his last match before retirement. Lowes was included in Bradford's 'Millennium Masters', 'Bull Masters', and in August 2007 was named in the club's 'Team of the Century'. Only six players have been included in all three lists; Lowes, [[Karl Fairbank]], [[Trevor Foster]], [[Keith Mumby]], [[Robbie Paul]] and [[Ernest Ward]].{{cn|date=January 2026}} ==Coaching career== ===Rugby league=== Lowes began a coaching career following his retirement as a player, joining [[Salford Red Devils|Salford City Reds]] as an assistant coach in 2004. Whilst on the coaching staff at Salford he made one appearance in a friendly match against [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]], scoring a try. Lowes later moved to [[Warrington Wolves]] as an assistant coach, and was named head coach following the departure of [[Paul Cullen (rugby league)|Paul Cullen]] in the 2008 season. After a disappointing opening to the 2009 season Lowes left the club and was replaced by [[Tony Smith (rugby league b. 1967)|Tony Smith]]. He later joined Leeds Rhinos as an assistant to [[Brian McDermott (rugby league)|Brian McDermott]]. In June 2014 Lowes was named head coach at Bradford Bulls. After the disappointment and shock of being relegated after [[Super League XIX]] after being one of the most successful Super League teams, Lowes led his team to one game away from returning to the Super League in 2016. They needed to win a 'Million Pound Game' and final qualifier against Wakefield, but suffered a 24-16 defeat which meant Bradford remained in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] for the 2016 season. Lowes after the match said he was tempted to quit rugby league.{{cite news |title = Million Pound Game: Bradford's James Lowes threatens to quit |publisher = BBC Sport |date = 2015-10-03 |url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/34434439 |access-date = 2015-10-03}} In August 2015, Lowes became assistant coach of the [[England national rugby league team|England national team]], becoming Steve McNamara's assistant.{{cite news |title = Bradford head coach takes England role |publisher = BBC Sport |date = 2015-08-08 |url = https://www.bbc.com/sport/0/rugby-league/33834375 |access-date = 2015-10-01}} Lowes helped England defeat New Zealand 2-1 in a three-test series in 2015. He then later left to take up teaching 14-19 year olds.{{cn|date=January 2026}} In July 2018 he returned to Leeds Rhinos as first team coach, after the departure of Brian McDermott. He became an assistant coach when [[David Furner]] took over in September 2018. He left the club in April 2019. In 2025 he joined Halifax Panthers, where he is a senior performance coach. ===Rugby union=== After retiring from playing rugby league, Lowes played amateur rugby union for West Park Leeds. In 2013 he was named head coach at rugby union club [[Leeds Tykes|Leeds Carnegie]], partner club of [[Leeds Rhinos]]. He left in June 2014 to join Bradford Bulls. He returned to the club, then renamed Yorkshire Carnegie, in 2016, initially as assistant to [[Bryan Redpath]], before becoming head coach in 2017. He left in June 2018. From 2019, Lowes spent two seasons with Ealing Trailfinders as a skills coach.
